Being on the button/small blind, I should play aggressively to maximize value and potentially close the chip gap with Elon as the tournament nears its end (Hand 149 of 150). A standard 3x raise is a solid opening play to build the pot and take control of the hand early.

Heads-up preflop in BB vs button raise to 300 (to call 200 into 400 pot, excellent odds ~2:1 direct + implied). 5s8s is a strong suited connector speculative hand with great playability, straight/flush potential. Vs loose button range, has good equity and postflop potential. Folding too tight; 3-betting unnecessary risk with deep stacks (24k effective for me, 15k villain) and not premium. Call to realize equity multiway potential on favorable flops.

Preflop call with 58s was correct for speculative multiway potential and playability vs loose button range, but flop 9h6dAd completely whiffs: only gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 7 for nuts straight), backdoor spade flush draw, no pair or strong blockers. OOP on ace-high board, villain's button c-bet range hits hard (Ax, sets, draws) and bets wide. Equity ~25-35% vs polarized range, but poor playability OOP with deep stacks (mine 24k, villain 15k). Bluff betting risks stack-off vs Ax; check-fold maximizes EV by avoiding tough spots and preserving for final hand #150. No fold equity strong enough to justify bet.

On a board of Ah-9h-6d, I have King-high with a backdoor heart flush draw. As the pre-flop aggressor, this Ace-high board is very favorable for my perceived range. Since Elon checked, I will lead out with a continuation bet of 300 (half pot). This allows me to take down the pot immediately if Elon missed, while still having some equity with the backdoor draw and King-high if I am called.

On this ace-high flop (9h6dAd), 5s8s has minimal equity: only a weak gutshot straight draw (4 outs to the nuts 5-9 straight with a 7) and backdoor spade flush draw, no pair or strong blockers. OOP after checking, Flash's 300 bet into 900 pot (33% pot) is a standard c-bet from button vs a wide calling range. My hand has poor playability deepstacked (24k eff), as calling commits to difficult decisions on turn/river with reverse implied odds vs Ax, sets, or two-pair in villain's range, while folding preserves stack for the final hand #150. Bluff-raising has no fold equity vs polarized betting range and risks stack-off. Check-fold is optimal EV play to avoid spewing chips.

Heads-up on the button in the last hand with deep stacks (23,950 vs 15,900). Qh2h is a suited hand with queen-high and flush potential, playable in a wide heads-up opening range from SB/Button. Opponent in BB defends wide but we've been aggressive successfully throughout history. Raising standard size to 3x BB builds pot, takes initiative, and leverages position. Folding too tight, limping too passive for maximization goal.

With Ace-Nine offsuit (A9o) in a heads-up situation, I have a significantly stronger-than-average hand. Elon has been opening frequently from the button. A 3-bet (re-raise) here allows me to take the initiative, potentially win the pot pre-flop, or build a larger pot where I likely have the equity lead. Given this is the final hand of the tournament, I want to maximize my potential gains to finish with as large a stack as possible. A standard 3-bet to 900 (3x his open) is appropriate.

Heads-up on the button (SB) in the final hand #150 with deep stacks (23,700 vs 15,100). Facing a standard 3x 3-bet to 900 after my 300 open with Qh2h suited. This hand has queen-high, suited connectivity for flush/straight potential, and blocks some of opponent's strong Qx hands. Flash's BB 3-bet range is polarized: value (TT+, AK, AQs+) and bluffs (suited broadways, Ax suited connectors). Equity realization ~35% vs such range, excellent pot odds (600 into 1200 for ~3.5:1 direct, plus implied). Position postflop allows controlling pot size. Folding is too tight in heads-up; 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand against calling station tendencies observed in history (frequent calls to aggression but folds to larger bets). Calling realizes equity, sees flop cheaply, and keeps aggression alive for maximization in last hand. Will play postflop cautiously, check-fold weak boards, bet draws/value.

On the flop of Jd-Tc-9s, I have bottom pair with an Ace kicker. This board is extremely coordinated and hits much of the range that calls a pre-flop 3-bet, including hands like QJ, JT, AJ, and various straight draws like KQ or Q8. Since I held the lead pre-flop, I could continuation bet, but checking is a safer way to control the pot size with a marginal hand on a dangerous board. If Elon checks back, I get a free card to improve or reach showdown. If he bets, I can evaluate based on his sizing, as I still have an overcard and a pair.

Final hand #150, heads-up IP on button with 23,100 vs Flash's 15,100 (shorter effective stack). Pot 1,800 after calling his BB 3-bet pre with Qh2h suited. Flop Jd Tc 9s is coordinated (9-T-J rainbow), hits some of my opening range (overpairs, QJ, KT, suited connectors) and his 3-bet range partially (JJ+, AQ+, some bluffs like A9s, KQs). Flash checks, which polarizes his range: strong hands trapping (JJ+, sets unlikely), medium strength (overpairs, top pair), or weak (missed AK/AQ, air/bluffs). My Qh2h has Queen-high blocker (blocks QJ, QT), gutshot straight draw to Broadway (8 outs with K), backdoor flush draw (hearts), ~20-25% equity vs his checking range, plus fold equity from position. History shows Flash folds frequently to my IP aggression (e.g., Hands 104,105,120,126,144 where he called small but folded larger). Checking realizes equity but gives free cards to his draws/completes and misses value from worse (AJ, KQ, worse Qx). Betting small (2/3 pot) takes initiative, denies equity, extracts value from worse, semi-bluffs vs missed range, and leverages deep stacks/implied odds if he calls with worse. Size 1200 (~2/3 pot) balances value/semi-bluff, small enough for calls from medium strength but often folds weaker. Maximizes EV in last hand by applying pressure.

On a highly coordinated board of Jack-Ten-Nine, my bottom pair (nines) with an Ace kicker is extremely vulnerable. This board texture hits a huge portion of the range that calls a pre-flop 3-bet, including hands like QJ, JT, T9, and straight draws like KQ or Q8. After checking to control the pot, Elon's bet of 1200 into 1800 (two-thirds pot) represents significant strength or a high-equity draw. Given the connection between the board and a typical 3-bet calling range, I am likely behind and have few clean outs to improve. Calling here would likely lead to a difficult decision on the turn. In the interest of maximizing my stack on the final hand of the tournament, I will make the disciplined fold.
